context.region: Gulf of America, East Breaks Block 602

actions_taken: Inspection of crane boom performed - no damage found
Onsite investigation performed on 22-May-2025 by BSEE
Pictures taken of broken lever hoist and chain
event.summary: On 21-May-2025 at approximately 1:45 p.m., a crane incident occurred on the Nansen Spar platform. Contract scaffold builders attached multiple 1/4-ton lever hoists to the crane boom for scaffolding stabilization without notifying the Lifting Authority. When the crane operator lifted the boom out of its rest after lunch break without conducting a pre-use inspection, one lever hoist broke and fell approximately 30 feet to the flare boom mezzanine deck. No injuries occurred. The incident resulted from communication failures, inadequate supervision, and simultaneous operations in a confined area.
recommendations: Verification of Temporary Anchor Points: The Lifting Authority will verify all temporary anchor points used for rigging prior to lifting operations
Control of Work Update: The control of work procedures will be updated for the lifting and rigging section, requiring the Lifting Authority to sign off on the approval of anchor points
Development of HSE Alert: An HSE alert will be developed to outline the failures that contributed to the incident, along with the corrective actions taken
Policy Update for Rigging and Lifting: The rigging and lifting policy will be updated to mandate inspections of the crane whenever the boom is placed into the boom rest
key_phrases: failed to notify the Anadarko Lifting Authority
resumed lifting operations without conducting another pre-use inspection
before the Crane Operator could react, a 1/4-ton lever hoist broke
Should have been locked out and tagged out
Insufficient knowledge and training among third-party personnel

hazards.name: Crane boom movement with attached equipment

threats.name: Unauthorized attachment of equipment to crane boom
Crane operation without adequate pre-use inspection
Failure to communicate work activities between contractors

controls.name: Lifting Authority notification and approval for anchor points
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring approval before attaching equipment to crane
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to prevent unauthorized modifications

supporting_text: contract scaffolding builders failed to notify the Anadarko Lifting Authority of the installed equipment
Contract scaffold builder supervisor should have notified the lifting authority of its intention to use the crane boom as a connection point
According to lessee policy, permission is required before attaching any items to the crane boom

controls.name: Pre-use crane inspection after breaks/interruptions
controls.barrier_role: Inspection to identify hazards before crane operation

supporting_text: the Crane Operator resumed lifting operations without conducting another pre-use inspection or checking the crane and boom for any factors that could affect performance
According to Anadarko policy the crane operator is only required to perform a pre-use once a day
There is no requirement to perform additional pre-use inspections if the crane is left unattended

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting and JSP/JSEA documentation
controls.barrier_role: Communication and planning to identify hazards and work methods
controls.lod_basis: Planning and hazard identification before work

supporting_text: Pre-job safety meeting with all personnel should have mentioned the use of the 1/4-ton lever hoist and the intention to use the crane boom as an anchor point
The contract scaffold builder's JSP mentions the use of a 'come along for cantilever for scaffolding,' but it is unclear where or how this would be used
Toolbox table talk, JSP, and JSEA's does not mention the use of a lever hoist to be used as a cantilever

controls.name: SIMOPS coordination and management
controls.barrier_role: Coordination of simultaneous operations to prevent conflicts
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory oversight of multiple concurrent activities

supporting_text: Simultaneous Operations (SIMOPS) - Contract scaffold builder, crane operations, and rigging operations were ongoing in a relatively small area
The Scaffolding Supervisor monitoring the work at that time also recalled seeing the scaffolding move and before he could call for an all stop, the lever hoist broke

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout procedures for crane boom
controls.barrier_role: Physical isolation to prevent equipment operation during work
controls.lod_basis: Energy isolation before work on equipment

supporting_text: Should have been locked out and tagged out

controls.name: Third-party personnel training on operator SOPs

supporting_text: Third party personnel are unfamiliar with lessee standard operating procedures
Insufficient knowledge and training among third-party personnel resulted in a failure to understand established standard operating procedures

controls.name: Visual communication and hand signals during crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Real-time communication to stop unsafe operations
controls.lod_basis: Active monitoring and intervention during operations

supporting_text: As soon as personnel on the deck and the scaffolding crew realized the crane boom was moving, they tried to stop the crane operation using hand signals (waving of the hands), but before the Crane Operator could react, a 1/4-ton lever hoist broke
The crane operator who seen personnel working under the crane boom should have mentioned to them he was going to lift the boom out of the cradle

controls.name: Equipment capacity and load rating compliance
controls.barrier_role: Equipment design to withstand expected loads

supporting_text: the contract scaffold builders used multiple 1/4-ton lever hoists as bracing, angled at 90 degrees to the crane boom for the 45-degree scaffolding supports
causing one of the 1/4-ton lever hoists to break
At the time of the incident a 1/4-ton lever hoist was damaged, the load chain broke approximately 2-3 foot up from the load hook

controls.name: Personnel positioning and exclusion zones
controls.barrier_role: Physical separation of personnel from dropped object hazard

supporting_text: workers were near the lever hoist when it broke
part of the 1/4-ton lever hoist fell approximately 30 feet to the flare boom mezzanine deck
we entered the data into a Dropped Object Consequences calculator, which indicated a slight potential consequence

controls.name: Emergency stop procedures and all-stop declaration
controls.barrier_role: Immediate cessation of operations to prevent escalation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to stop further damage

supporting_text: Eventually, the Crane Operator halted crane operations, and an all stop was declared
An inspection of the crane boom was performed and no damage was found

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
